source_label: Wikipedia — Talk:Nostr (Pronunciation section documents the long-o vs ostrich split)
notes: Decentralized social protocol; the name is an acronym for 'Notes and Other Stuff Transmitted by Relays' — so it has no vowel and no official spoken form. Two camps: (1) NOH-ster with a long o like 'nose' (matching the 'Notes...' expansion), and (2) NOSS-ter with the short o of 'ostrich'. The ostrich reading is widespread enough that Nostr users nickname themselves 'nostriches', strong community evidence for the short-o variant. Wikipedia's Talk page logs both variants (nōstər, nôstər); creator fiatjaf has explicitly told people to 'invent your own way', so there is no creator decree and it is genuinely contested. Stress on the first syllable either way. (Other heard-in-the-wild forms include No-stir and Naw-ster.)
